original:
(0,it.zI)(n||(n=(0,et.Z)(["Beneath your ship, you stand atop a glassy surface that must have once been sand. Apocalyptic nuclear fire long ago scoured it to smooth reflectivity. Your Codex ticks softly with faint background radiation - not enough to concern your body’s enhanced repair and immune systems, but enough to remind you of the danger inherent to this beautiful, bombed-out wasteland.

		The gleaming surface stretches in every direction, though cracks spiderweb through to the south.

		Much further north lies a crater... and beyond another - large enough to swallow a city whole and yet hunger for more."])))

(0,it.zI)(a||(a=(0,et.Z)(["You note faint ripples in the surface of the nuclear-smoothed glass as you explore. At first, they appear to stretch in a linear east-west manner, but as you observe more distant surroundings, you detect a northward arc to their form: these are massive ripples - not straight lines. The blast that carved a massive crater on the horizon struck with such force that it writ the power of its shockwaves into the very surface of the glass.\n\n\t\tA few rocks peek through the glass to west, their northern faces strangely smoothed out like candles left too close to a hearth."])))

(0,it.zI)(i||(i=(0,et.Z)(["The glass to the south opens into a jagged crevasse. Unsupported from below, the surface layer appears to have collapsed deep into the bottom of the pit - presenting a fatally-spiky landing for anyone or anything unlucky enough to tumble in. Unbroken glass stretches away to the north, east, and west."])))

(0,it.zI)(s||(s=(0,et.Z)(['The glass runs alongside a small crevasse to the north - just wide and jagged enough to block any attempts at traditional locomotion. It appears that the underlying sediment eroded away, leaving the glass "plate" to collapse in under its own weight - and create a pit of spiky death. More than one set of animal bones decorates the bottom like grisly trophies.\n\n\t\tThe glass to the southeast is more fractured than elsewhere, riven with spiderweb-like inclusions of white.'])))

(0,it.zI)(r||(r=(0,et.Z)(["White moss springs out of cracks between the greenish glass, proving that not even an impermeable ceramic layer can choke off life. Your Codex’s sensors report the moss to be nontoxic and less radioactive than the surrounding material. Life... finds a way.
		
		This strange plant seems to thrive more to the east, south, and west of you. To the north, the glass is unbroken and smooth.
		
		You also notice a burned-out starship hull to the southwest, though judging by its state, any salvage was likely stripped away long ago."])))

(0,it.zI)(h||(h=(0,et.Z)(["The smooth glass you parked your ship on gives way to patternless cracking here, splitting into smaller and smaller pieces to the east and south. In the gaps between, a whitish moss has grown in, puffing up softly. At a distance, it looks almost like a spider’s web, woven into the ground itself.\n\t\t\n\t\tUnbroken glass tantalizes you to the north. The webs continue in every other direction - save for the immediate south, where a burned-out starship hull sits in the way."])))

(0,it.zI)(u||(u=(0,et.Z)(["A simple shack is standing alongside the edge of the wrecked ship to the southeast. The walls appear to be made from laser-perforated bulkheads - for a breezy interior, perhaps! Salvaged tabletops form the roof, held on with rusted, immobilized machine clamps. Radioactive glass makes for privacy windows, a feature undercut by the complete destruction of the door—now blown in half, painted in scorch marks, and overgrown with white moss.
		
		Someone was living and scrapping here... until it came to an unfortunate end.
		
		Highly radioactive sludge impedes any travel to the north and west, but the mossy ground makes for easy moving to the east and south."])))

(0,it.zI)(l||(l=(0,et.Z)(["Phaedra II’s glassy surface threads between the boulders to the north and a pool of caustic slime to the southwest. A few splinters of rock linger here, shed by their traumatized brethren to the north, waiting for a chance to stub your [pc.toe]. The glass continues to the east and west. You can squeeze through north through the boulders, but there’s no reason to venture southward into that sludge."])))

(0,it.zI)(d||(d=(0,et.Z)(["You stand amidst a number of building-sized boulders with their northern faces half-melted into what some might consider art. Phaedra’s surface is truly unique among the cosmos for its beautiful desolation - or so you hope. This level of nuclear devastation shouldn’t be repeated - any more than history already has.\n\nThe rocky behemoths hem you in to the west, but you can squeeze north into their midst. A glassy green expanse stretches off to the east and west."])))

(0,it.zI)(c||(c=(0,et.Z)(["At last, you’re given a break from treading on Phaedra’s hardened surface - by the selfsame moss you’ve been observing for the last little while. The soil must be especially fertile beneath the glass, because it has completely overgrown the bounds it exists within elsewhere. The springy alabaster foliage cushions your movements and emits a faint, sweet fragrance. You feel like this would be a nice place to rest, if it weren’t for the ever-present danger of scrappers, mutants, and worse."])))

(0,it.zI)(g||(g=(0,et.Z)(["Spores erupt out of the whitish moss when you walk, bursting into the air like harmless snow. They swirl about on the tainted air, riding the breeze till they can find a new piece of ground to colonize—a bit like the UGC, you suppose.\n\t\t\n\t\tThe path threads north and east between impassible mountains, a crashed ship, and radioactive sludge. Further north, the terrain is less obstructed."])))

(0,it.zI)(y||(y=(0,et.Z)(["This place is a literal graveyard - and not just for the ship that smashed itself nose-down in the ground to the north after being riddled with holes. Someone took the time to gather the crew’s bodies and pile them beneath a cairn of metal, glass, and moss. Two beat-up spears stab into the ground alongside it, each with a rusty cog strapped on.
		
		Someone took the time to fish out the bodies and give them a proper burial. The moss now growing over the cairn lends the whole affair an unsettling energy.
		
		The moss-webbed ground stretches around the wreck to the northeast and northwest. The south is still too radioactive to explore, and swampy to boot."])))

(0,it.zI)(p||(p=(0,et.Z)(['On the southern edge of "the webs," you come across an oddity: a missing chunk of glass. The pentagon-shaped hole in the ground has been filled entirely with the whitish plants that live here, softer than grass and thrice as dense.\n\t\t\n\t\tIt’s a shame the terrain gets sludgy and more radioactive to the south. Not much could survive out there - and you certainly could not without making a whole expedition out of it.'])))

(0,it.zI)(m||(m=(0,et.Z)(["As you move onto a fresh, glassy plate, tiny worms lift out of the surrounding moss, wiggling in the air like stalks of agitated corn. They’re only two or three inches tall, and when you lean down for a better look, they vanish back into the moss. Strange.
		
		A dilapidated spaceship sits embedded in the ground to the west. Every scrap of usable material has already been picked off, and what’s left is a rusted heap. The moss grows extra thick around the base.
		
		This plant-webbed region continues to the north, east, and south."])))

(0,it.zI)(f||(f=(0,et.Z)(["The webs of white moss died off here, blackened by rot and desiccation. When your [pc.foot] brushes against some, it crackles apart like sun-baked plastic wrap. No apparent cause presents itself. Something chemical could have been poured into the spiderwebbing network of soil, you suppose. In a toxic wasteland like this, it would be nearly impossible which life-obliterating condition is responsible.\n\t\t\n\t\tYour Codex’s Geiger counter goes crazy whenever you aim it south or east, not that there’s much in that direction aside from more flats all the way to distant, faintly glowing mountains."])))

(0,it.zI)(w||(w=(0,et.Z)(["A factory used to stand here - you’re sure of it. Bits of the foundation still jut through the ground’s glassy cap, melted into distinct ceramic stalagmites all their own. Bits of metal so rusted that they look like they have reddish-brown fur are piled together beside the foundation. Someone picked over these ruins already, salvaged the valuables, and left the debris in a pile.\n\t\t\n\t\tSpiderwebbing white moss stretches through the cracked glass in all directions, though the patch to the south looks darkened with decay."])))

(0,it.zI)(b||(b=(0,et.Z)(["Spiderwebbing patterns of moss grow through the myriad cracks in the glassy ground below, thickening to the south and east. Things die off a bit to the north and west, where the glass is less broken and life struggles to take hold among the few opportunities that present themselves.\n\t\t\n\t\tFrom time to time, you even see some bugs scurrying around, weaving in lines from subterranean colony to subterranean colony like ants on the march—only these ants have ten legs and bizarre double-abdomens. Maybe they’re mutants, but maybe they’re mere aliens."])))

(0,it.zI)(k||(k=(0,et.Z)(["Phaedra’s bombed-out surface may seem scoured of life at first glance, but your Codex’s sensors tell a different story. A rich biome of anaerobic, radiation-resistant bacteria thrive beneath the sealed-off surface. Above, you even manage to catch sight of a bat-like bird—or is that a bird-like bat? No matter. The planet doesn’t care if its inhabitants nuked themselves to pieces; even then, there’s still plenty of life around judging by tread-shaped cracks in the glassy ground."])))

(0,it.zI)(v||(v=(0,et.Z)(['Petals of greenish glass curl with flower-like concentricity near large cracks in Phaedra’s surface, their gem-like edges tinting from green to a near luminous pink. Though they lack stems, there’s something more to them than mere minerals - enough for your Codex to flag them for potential future study. Their edges are certainly sharp enough for you to stick to the clear spaces between "flower" bunches.\n\n\t\t\tMore of the unclassified exotic "growths" to the north and east. Smoother glass stretches out to the south and west.'])))

(0,it.zI)(T||(T=(0,et.Z)(["Green-blue wasteland stretches out in all directions, but you presently stand amidst a number of gem-studded rents in the otherwise wavy blue glass. The growths look a bit like flowers - their petals at least, collected in bunches that you might be happy to consider as flora, though for all you know they’re the result of a chemical interaction with the acidic precipitation Phaedra calls rain.\n\n\t\t\tThe beautifully-fringed patches continue to the north and south of here."])))

(0,it.zI)(A||(A=(0,et.Z)(["Phaedra may have had its biosphere burned away in a tide of nuclear fire, but the crystalline, flower-like growths springing out of nearby rents hint at new, life-like processes boldly carving spaces amidst the devastation. Scans struggle to pinpoint the glittering, concentric growths as living flora or the result of natural chemical processes. They may remain a puzzle for future scientists in the years to come.

			Going north is impossible. A sheer, darkened rock juts out of the surface like a sword from a wound.

			Strange green flames flicker to the east, waving like bushes in the breeze. More of them beautiful-but-prickly flowers sprout to the south. Westward, the ground itself seems to be cracked and heaved as if by a mighty explosion."])))

(0,it.zI)(S||(S=(0,et.Z)(["Radiation Warning! The Codex won’t stop giving you alarms about the area to the north and northwest. Nothing up there seems worth investigating anyhow; it’s all flat, blasted glass.\n\n\t\t\tThe immediate area is similarly flattened, with only the faintest rolls and ripples disturbing the glossy surface below. Traversal to the south and west leads to similar terrain, untainted by Codex alarms about things like ‘lethality’ and ‘certain death’. Flickering green flames haunt the area to the east, the origins unclear."])))

(0,it.zI)(I||(I=(0,et.Z)(["If you squint, Phaedra’s green gently rolling surface could almost be mistaken for grass. Of course, grass doesn’t crackle like ice when you step too hard, and actually cushions any [pc.footNoun]-falls. Phaedra doesn’t. It’s been hardened in atomic fire and tempered to unyielding firmness.\n\n\t\t\tThe rolling emerald plains stretch off to the north. To the east, you can spot strangely flickering green fires. South is blocked by a sheer boulder, seemingly extruded from below by enormous pressure. To the west, the green ground has been cracked and broken but remains passable."])))

(0,it.zI)(Y||(Y=(0,et.Z)(["Your Codex’s alarms start pinging like crazy the farther north you step: Radiation warning! It chirps like a bothersome bird, but you know well enough to trust it. Phaedra’s glassy surface may be even smoother up there, but there isn’t a single visible landmark worth investigating. Anything that once existed in that space must have been obliterated by a point-blank impact from a fission bomb. It would be less radioactive to the south, along the rim a of small, jagged crater. West follows the crater’s lip along the irradiated zone. East offers a smoother path on the glass."])))

(0,it.zI)(E||(E=(0,et.Z)(["Iron-rich soil, crystallized by heat, crunches underneath you as you walk, threading your way between stouter shards that might actually deal you damage. A deep crater filled with even sharper rocks looms below to the west. You keep your distance, unwilling to risk toppling into the nuclear-induced geological disturbance. The crater stretches south and twists away to the northwest. The north and east lead to more of the glassy plain you’ve come to expect."])))

(0,it.zI)(x||(x=(0,et.Z)(["You stand on the edge of a jagged, foreboding crater. Rusty brown rocks, sharpened by the force of being dislodged from the earth at explosive speed, threaten to rip at your [pc.feet] with every movement. Descent into the pit might be possible, but the danger and lack of any perceivable reward make it seem a fool’s errand. The crater’s lip extends to the north and southwest. Directly south, the terrain grows glassy once more. Eastward lies a strange feature - crystalline-seeming flowers?"])))

(0,it.zI)(H||(H=(0,et.Z)(["You stand on the border between geological features. Here things are green, glassy, and smoothed aesthetically by the flame of nuclear fire. To the north, the terrain is more reddish brown, awash in hard-edged rocks with heavy coats of oxidation. The rolling glassy plains continue to the south, but in the east something sparkles atop the strange surface."])))

(0,it.zI)(C||(C=(0,et.Z)(["Phaedra’s glassy surface ripples just enough to make traversing it a tremendous pain. It stretches on in all directions, though craters to the northwest and more distant north provide ample locales to gather your bearings from.\n\t\t\n\t\tYou’re pretty sure you can still see your ship parked off to the south-south-west."])))

(0,it.zI)(R||(R=(0,et.Z)(["Nuclear-baked glass crunches beneath you as you move through the wasteland. Huge boulders pile up to the west, their northern faces liquefied and refused into a pudding-like appearance. Just north, the glass splits apart around a crater. More sprawling, glassy ripples undulate away to the east and south, leading on to more signs of Phaedra’s molten past."])))

(0,it.zI)(N||(N=(0,et.Z)(["Whoah! You come upon a deep, jagged pit. The interior is filled with massive, reddish-brown spikes of impure iron ore. The ground around the pit is similarly ruddy and littered with smaller, spiky fragments. You’ll have to step carefully on your way around it.

There doesn’t seem to be anything worth investigating at the bottom aside from a nasty case of Tetanus.

Rocks with their northern faces melted into semi-liquid smoothness stand to the west. To the east and south, Phaedra transitions back to its green-glassed ground."])))

(0,it.zI)(B||(B=(0,et.Z)(["Radiation Warning! Your Codex insists you not step further into the flat glass to the north. The point-blank impact of a fission bomb has left a wide swath of the terrain barren, uninhabitable, and unworthy of your attention. To the south lies a jagged pit surrounded by shards of iron-rich rocks, as reddish-brown as dried blood. A quick scan from your Codex identifies the remnants of an underground aquifer explosively vaporized during the planet’s apocalyptic bombardment. Smooth glass surface stretches off to the east and west, offering a less perilous place to stand."])))

(0,it.zI)(L||(L=(0,et.Z)(["Radiation Warning! Your Codex demands you avoid traveling to the north and west - both areas are too contaminated by radiation to safely explore, even with your blood fully infused by self-repairing microsurgeons.\n\nIf you head east or south, you’ll be stepping on jagged chunks of rust-red earth - apparently explosively excavated from the matching pit to the southeast."])))

(0,it.zI)(O||(O=(0,et.Z)(["Straddling between intense radiation to the west and a pit of metallic, heat-hardened blades of earth to the east, you’ll have to [pc.move] carefully. The pit itself appears empty save for a few unfortunate skeletons that fell - or were tossed - in. It’s not worth the effort and danger of climbing into it yourself. You can follow the lip of the crater south from here, or step north onto a stretch of smooth glass once more."])))

(0,it.zI)(_||(_=(0,et.Z)(["You stand amongst shards of ruddy earth, as if blasted out of the pit to the east. Most of them are small and fragile enough to shatter underfoot. The rest make you step carefully during your exploration of this alien planet. The pit itself is even worse - absolutely filled with nightmare-like spires of jagged iron formations. You can follow the pit to the north, or work your way to southeast to examine its southern edge.\n\nRocks with melt-softened faces loom to the south. Westward, you spot what looks like a tree, half-blasted to oblivion and standing all the same."])))

(0,it.zI)(G||(G=(0,et.Z)(["Boulders would be one thing you’d expect to handle a nuclear blast better than most, and you suppose these have - by remaining standing in spite of the apocalyptic forces that washed over them not so long ago. Their northern faces clearly liquified for a moment, long enough to leave them looking mushy and soft as mud. A touch confirms they’re still rocks, however much their surface resembles a sloppy mess.\n\nYou can’t squeeze through the dense westward stone, but travel south through the melty boulders remains an option - as does heading north or east to the edge of a jagged crater."])))

(0,it.zI)(j||(j=(0,et.Z)(["You stand amidst a field of semi-melted boulders. Your Codex’s geological analysis function indicates that this may have once been the final resting place of a glacier. It’s strange to think that ice and snow did more to move these behemoths than the nuclear fire that scarred their faces into runny messes.\n\nOnce-melted stones stand to the north and south from here. To the east is more of the green, glassy surface you’ve come to expect from Phaedra."])))

(0,it.zI)(D||(D=(0,et.Z)(["Your Codex’s Geiger counter chirps a little faster here, warning you of more radiation to the south and west - not that those impassible nuke-blasted mountains are likely to be worth exploring. To the north, the ground’s glassy surface shifts hues through whole rainbows of purple and green shades in a truly beautiful display of desolation."])))

(0,it.zI)(z||(z=(0,et.Z)(["Green gives way to blue, purple, even reds and oranges here! The nuclear-fired glass in this area must cross over numerous geological layers with their own exotic mineral inclusions, resulting in a magical-seeming wonderland of fantastic colors. The unsubtle beeps of your Codex’s geiger counter partly ruin the illusion. You can follow this road of rainbows north, or go south into the expanse of pure emerald.\n\nSheer irradiated mountains cut you off to the west, and densely packed boulders block travel east."])))

(0,it.zI)(M||(M=(0,et.Z)(["The kaleidoscopic waves of colored glass are almost dizzying to behold. Without the surface cracks and blemishes, you’d look to be standing on an undulating palette of brilliant color. It is truly beautiful, but a sterile kind of beautiful - like the twinkling void of space or the rolling dunes of a picturesque yet barren desert.\n\nThis colorful glass stretches off to the north, south, and west. Huge rocks with their northern faces half-melted block off any attempts to journey to the east."])))

(0,it.zI)(P||(P=(0,et.Z)(["The nuclear-melted glass here is different than elsewhere - more vibrant. Awash with hues from the most neon pink to the deepest blues, the glass below feels like a road of rainbow, laid out just for you to [pc.move] upon. A huge chunk of rock flanks the colorful path’s west. On the east, dense stones with the north faces half-melted hem you in. What looks like a single tree lies a short walk to the north. More rainbows wind south.\n\nYour geiger counter clicks a steady tempo - enough radiation to monitor, but not enough to be concerned about in any meaningful way."])))

(0,it.zI)(F||(F=(0,et.Z)(["You stop at a tree - the only one on Phaedra you’ve seen","! It’s not alive, not by a long shot. The bark is entirely missing from the north face, and both the wood itself and the remaining bits of bark have the whitish-gray look of stone. Indeed, it feels like rock to your touch, and your Codex confirms: this tree is petrified in place, calcified into a stony sentinel by the combination of mineral-rich biology and the searing indifference of nuclear fire.\n\nTo the east lies a path circling around a jagged, red-brown pit. To the south are more glassy plains, though green you’ve grown accustomed to gives way to rainbows of beautiful hues. Westward leads to more of that plain, crunchy green glass."])),"Phaedra: The Wastes"===getCurrentRoom().planet?" - outside of the distant Radwoods":"")

(0,it.zI)(q||(q=(0,et.Z)(["Wedged up against a scorched rock face, you stand on a winding expanse of multi-colored glass. Created by a variety of sub-surface minerals melted into the melange during the blast, this particular part of Phaedra’s surface shines with its own strange beauty. The colorful glass ground offers passage to the north, along the mountainside, and to the east, toward some melty boulders."])))

(0,it.zI)(W||(W=(0,et.Z)(["Here lies the border between a patch of Phaedra’s typical, greenish-glassed ground, and a more colorful segment of rippling waves of rainbow-hued ceramic. Unlike a rainbow, the colors eschew any particularly pattern or structure. Purple blends directly into orange and straight into blue a step later, dictated by the unique chemical composition of the soil underground.\n\t\t\nA deeper scan with your Codex indicates that you may stand over an industrial dumping ground - each color matched to a unique pollutant."])))

(0,it.zI)(V||(V=(0,et.Z)(["The immediate north also holds something far more intriguing than any geological feature: several pieces of downed spaceships, all massively scorched as if struck by an immense barrage. Some appear to have residual power coursing through their circuits. Bigger wrecks lie beyond them - larger, almost intact hulks with clearly operational turrets still mounted. Furrows in the ground indicate that they were towed here and positioned in their current location.\n\t\t\t\n\t\t\tTo the south, the emerald ground transitions into shimmering, iridescent bans that shift in hue - stark in their ghostly beauty. Meanwhile, the remnants of a tree somehow stand upright to the east. It’s as gray as the boulders to the southeast."])))

(0,it.zI)(U||(U=(0,et.Z)(["There’s definitely an order to how the shipwrecks are arranged. Whole vessels and larger pieces are sorted northward, smaller pieces and more brutally detonated ships spread out to the south like dragon’s teeth, effectively preventing approach by wider, armored vehicles. Many hulks still bear their original weapon emplacements too. Some of the huge turrets are still actively sweeping back and forth across the blasted earth, sensors feeding their views to an unseen operator. South leads away from this spacey junkyard, but the other direction holds a tempting structure of its own - a vertically-embedded cruiser that looks disturbingly intact for how deeply its nose is buried in the ground."])))

(0,it.zI)(Z||(Z=(0,et.Z)(["You stand in the shadow of a Pyrite Heavy Industries cruiser, though instead of parking in a stately manner, it’s been slammed nose first into the ground deeply enough to hold its entire aft end upright, like a modest skyscraper from a coreward city. It clearly suffered as much as the rest of this fallen junkyard during its plunge to the planet, but someone has been effecting repairs quite effectively. You can’t spot a single hole in the hull, though plenty of half-scrubbed scorch marks remain.\n\n\t\t\tThe airlock door has even been reworked, detached from its original mooring and rotated ninety degrees to align with Phaedra’s surface. Above it hangs the sort of sign you’d expect to see in a storefront: “Open.” ",""])),window.rummiHostile()?"It is unlit, the store closed.":"It has remained brightly lit since your ride here with Gayll.")

(0,it.zI)(K||(K=(0,et.Z)(["One tree stands before you, alone in this blasted-out wasteland. You aren’t sure how the twisted, knobby plant survived Phaedra’s nuclear fire when so much else did not, or how it’s managing to keep its bouquet of weakened leaves aloft, but it is. This thing saw nuclear fire and lived to tell about it.\n\t\t\n\t\tThe white moss in the ground grows all over its roots, filling every crack and crevice with alabaster. It’s thicker between the glass plates here than elsewhere."])))

(0,it.zI)(J||(J=(0,et.Z)(["What’s this? A water collector? Rebar posts driven into the soft moss support a jerry-rigged water collector. A cast-iron bucket hangs beneath sheets of clear plastic, enclosed in such a way that morning dew and atmospheric humidity can be condensed for usable drinking water.

		Tire tracks running east-west alongside the collector indicate that this may be a piece of permanent infrastructure for one of Phaedra’s nomadic groups.

		The webs of white moss grow extra thick and lush here, happily drinking down every drop of spilled liquid."])))

(0,it.zI)(Q||(Q=(0,et.Z)(["Flowers sprout from Phaedra’s greenish, glassy crust here - or at least something that resembles flowers. The petals curl out of cracks in the glass, beautiful, curved, and razor-sharp. There aren’t stems like you might see on a tulip or rose, just a concentric pattern of curved, jutting danger.\n\n\t\t\tThere’s a stark beauty to your surroundings, even if it makes you doublecheck where you place your [pc.feet]. More of these “flowers” sprout to the north. Things are flatter and smoother to the west. A structure rises to the south, and something resembling a giant beast’s thigh-bone juts from Phaedra’s blasted surface to the east."])))

(0,it.zI)(X||(X=(0,et.Z)(["Crystalline growths line the nuke-smoothed ground where its less-than-sturdy crust has fractured. The Codex’s scanners seem too puzzled to mark them as organic or mineral. It flags them for further study post-Rush, once Phaedra has been fully integrated into the U.G.C. proper.\n\n\t\t\tYou move carefully around the razor-sharp petals while considering your options: flat glass to the north and east, or more faceted flowers to the south and west."])))

(0,it.zI)($||($=(0,et.Z)(["Fission-baked glass shifts and groans softly as you move around atop it’s flash-fried surface. This desolation ought to be captured on film to show for use as an educational tool across the galaxy. This is what mutually-assured destruction doctrines bring. This is where nuclear grandstanding comes to rest once power falls into the wrong hands, as it inevitably does. If only the older Phaedrans had settled for rail cannons and orbital kinetic bombardment! Travel across the surface ripples is possible in all directions, though strange-looking crystal flowers are visible to the west, and ghostly green flames flicker in the breeze to the north."])))
(0,it.zI)(ee||(ee=(0,et.Z)(["You get the sense that there is a lot to learn about this location... in due time. (Editor's note: Description is TBD.)"])))
(0,it.zI)(te||(te=(0,et.Z)(["You get the sense that there is a lot to learn about this location... in due time. (Editor's note: Description is TBD.)"])))
(0,it.zI)(oe||(oe=(0,et.Z)(["You get the sense that there is a lot to learn about this location... in due time. (Editor's note: Description is TBD.)"])))
(0,it.zI)(ne||(ne=(0,et.Z)(["You get the sense that there is a lot to learn about this location... in due time. (Editor's note: Description is TBD.)"])))
(0,it.zI)(ae||(ae=(0,et.Z)(["You get the sense that there is a lot to learn about this location... in due time. (Editor's note: Description is TBD.)"])))
(0,it.zI)(ie||(ie=(0,et.Z)(["You get the sense that there is a lot to learn about this location... in due time. (Editor's note: Description is TBD.)"])))
(0,it.zI)(se||(se=(0,et.Z)(["You get the sense that there is a lot to learn about this location... in due time. (Editor's note: Description is TBD.)"])))
(0,it.zI)(re||(re=(0,et.Z)(["You get the sense that there is a lot to learn about this location... in due time. (Editor's note: Description is TBD.)"])))

(0,it.zI)(he||(he=(0,et.Z)(["Radiation warning! According to your Codex, the area to the east is completely uninhabitable, and more than risky to explore, even with your internal microsurgeons clearing away radioactive taint at capacity. It doesn’t have any such warnings about the area to the north, even though you see strange emerald fire flickering atop the glassy surface like bushes and trees shaped of living fire.\n\n\t\t\tThe plains to the south and west are more like what you’ve come to expect from this planet: largely flat, green, and thoroughly baked to a mirror finish."])))

(0,it.zI)(ue||(ue=(0,et.Z)(["Radiation warning! The Codex’s built-in geiger-counter won’t stop churring, particularly when pointed to the east. Things get uninhabitable that way pretty quick. Travel north, south, and west seems safe enough for now, though there appears to be a giant thigh bone jutting out of the ground to the south. Twinkling gemstones - or something close enough - are visible to the west."])))

(0,it.zI)(le||(le=(0,et.Z)(["From a distance, you might mistake the solitary tree trunk hanging onto the atom-blasted earth as some kind of giant’s bone, embedded halfway. The upper branches are long gone, and the trunk itself calcified as white as white as the skeletal material it resembles. It feels gritty to the touch and provides little shade.\n\n\t\t\tNorth are more glassy-looking plains. West lie a field of glassy-looking flowers, and to the south is what looks like some kind of rusted-out pyre - a site of some religious significance perhaps?"])))

(0,it.zI)(de||(de=(0,et.Z)(["You come upon a strange metal shrine. Welded-together exhaust pipes create a gracefully fluted structure, supporting a half dozen shopping carts assembled into a kind of net. Fire-blackened char mars the whole assembly. Beneath lies a pit, similarly blackened by recent fire and half-filled with ash.\n\n\t\tIts purpose is not immediately apparent, but you suspect it to be a place of religious significance, perhaps even some kind of scrapyard funeral pyre."])))

(0,it.zI)(Ge||(Ge=(0,et.Z)(['Ahead of you, just over the westernmost rise, is a lengthy concrete platform sprawling across the dunes. Intermittent pillars support what’s left of a roof hanging lopsided overhead, the clear victim of a mortar strike at some point in the past. Even still, the structure’s still in use: there are a couple dozen figures milling around on the platform, and the lights are still running in the little kiosk off to one side. \n\n\t\tA huge sign hangs above the stairs up onto the structure, reading "WELCOME TO SHINEGLASS STATION. ENJOY YOUR TRIP."'])))

Shineglass Station is less a building and more a gathering place beneath the shade of a roof -- it’s a fifty-yard-long, five-wide concrete platform in the middle of the wasteland that runs parallel to a maglev rail that against all odds is still intact and humming with power. The west end of the station’s been hit by an explosive, collapsing the roof and shattering a few yards of the platform, but otherwise the place has survived the Glass War relatively intact. There’s even a still-functioning kiosk on the east end of the station, beside which is an electronic ticket terminal.\n\nFolk of various races are standing around the place: bird-like taurs, leafy-green reptilians, fluffy caniforms, and even a couple of wary humans.
Ticket: Shieldrock

original:
(0,it.zI)(je||(je=(0,et.Z)(["Shieldrock Station is a long, raised concrete platform with a paneled roof overtop it. It’s similar to Shineglass in terms of construction, though it’s in markedly better repair, with an open ticket counter and refreshment kiosk. Everything here’s manned by DEE-SLs, much larger and more heavily armed than those serving on the train — these are hulking warrior machines ready to defend the realm of the Evermind to the death if need be.

		The armored train is parked here, currently undergoing rapid maintenance from a crew of cowled techno-clerics. Elcine is among their number, overseeing a crew working on the engine car.
		
		[flags.SHINEGLASS_ELYSTIEL_FREED|The birdy bard, Elystiel, is sitting more or less where you left her. She’s got her guitar out, strumming a much more peaceful, soothing melody than when you first met her. There’s a tin can in front of her, and every so often one of the passengers waiting by the train drops a bit of the local currency in with a satisfying clatter.]"])))
Ticket: Shineglass

original: Beyond the ticket kiosk is fare dodger detention.
It is empty at the moment, since you freed Hureya.

Hureya is still stuck in it, head down, ass up. She will remain stuck in it until she can work out a way past the DEE-SL’s logic loop, which given her disposition doesn’t seem will happen any time soon. Sucks to be her.

At the far end of the platform is a bizarre sight that takes you a few moments to get your head around. In a box demarcated in hazard yellow is some kind of metallic holding device, and bent over, head and paws trapped in it, is a big, naked hyrax. Her long, messy slice of blonde hair is vaguely familiar.
Pillory
The pillory stands empty; you freed the Warmistress.

original: You stand outside the fortified door to a decades old, pre-glass military base.
This room should never be entered. It’s only here to indicate further rooms inside, and the player should be seemlessly teleported first.

(0,it.zI)(Pe||(Pe=(0,et.Z)(["You’re on a flatbed truck hurtling through the dunes beside the train. A massive grapple launcher, something you’d more expect to see on an old ironclad sailing ship, has been bolted to the end of the truck. The line’s been ripped out by the VIP car falling away, and indeed half the bolts have been wrenched out. The whole thing might go flying if you touch it hard enough.\n\n\t\tThe wind’s roaring past you, and dust and sand are coming up in thick clouds to either side of you. The only way forward is with a leap, onto the container truck ahead of you in the convoy."])))

The hyraxi raiders you defeated are sprawled out on the floor, submissively licking their wounds. ","\n\n\t\tThe container doesn’t have a proper door to the front, but somebody’s hacksawed a door into it anyway, just covering it up with a draped blanket nailed to the top."])),1===flags.WAR_RIGS_2_STATE?"The boys have left some crates of supplies lying open, ripe for the picking.":"You’ve emptied out the crates here, leaving nothing for them to use against you.")

(0,it.zI)(We||(We=(0,et.Z)(["The gap between vehicles feels larger than it really is, the ground rushing past in the space between the unsteady and unsynchronized bounces of the trucks as they rattle across desert at speed. You crouch down, slowly waiting for the perfect moment to leap, fingers snatching the rungs of a ladder securely attached to the rear - a solid place to anchor whilst you swing open the rear door of the cargo hauler.

"])))

(0,it.zI)(Ve||(Ve=(0,et.Z)(["You’ve clambered up into the cabin of the artillery trailer-truck, mantling in beside a scared-shitless hyraxi boy who’s decided the best thing to do is just keep his paw on the peddle and keep driving, doing his best to ignore you. Ahead of you is a huge war-rig of a truck, from which comes the thundering, thumping bass that’s been assailing your ears since the attack began. There’s a tower of speakers all piled up on top of each other, surrounding a platform on which you can make out a few figures presumably playing DJ for the raiding party.\n\n\t\tYou can also see Johana, Elcine, and [rival.name] have made it nearly halfway up the train. The gunslinger’s exchanging punches with a particularly brawny hyraxi boy while Elcine’s clearing a path around her with that massive wrench-staff. [rival.name]’s contributing a little too, occasionally poking out from behind Johana to snap off a shot with a pocket pistol."])))
Jump!

(0,it.zI)(Ue||(Ue=(0,et.Z)(["You leap from the artillery truck’s cabin and onto the back of the massive stack of speakers at the back of the next. The bass pounding off it is so loud that even before you jumped you could feel it in your bones - now that you’re clambering up the stack, it’s physically deafening, even drowning out the cavalcade of gunshots and crashing steel echoing from the train at your side. Even the roaring motors of the surrounding war-buggies are just a din in the back of your mind, pushed aside by the banshee’s howl of electric guitar and thundering drums.\n\n\t\t\tIt’s enough to make you start humming along with the tune."])))

(0,it.zI)(Ze||(Ze=(0,et.Z)(["You’re aboard the Warmistress’s very own Thunder Rig, the howling banshee at the head of the armored convoy attacking the train. Dozens of buggies and bikes are circling you menacingly, but with the defeat of their battle-bard, the furor’s gone out of the boys by and large. They aren’t making swipes at you directly now, at least, and there’s a clear shot from the grapple cannon on this rig to the train again."])))

(0,it.zI)(Ke||(Ke=(0,et.Z)(["This car’s dedicated to holding the lower-class passengers’ various belongings: suitcases, oversized backpacks, a few gun cases and scooters - anything that can’t fit into the under-seat stowage provided to them.

		There’s also a section marked off for parking a motorbike inside, but it’s instead currently [flags.SHINEGLASS_JOHANA_IN_PARTY|occupied by Howinn Creed, Johana’s wife, who’s currently bound up in bondage ropes. For safe transport, she assures you.|full of an extraordinarily buxom leithan hermaphrodite in a substantial amount of bondage rope.]

		Three DEE-SLs are lying in pieces on the floor in here, two smashed apart by some tremendous force and the other slashed to pieces by a vicious blade. There’s a couple of unconscious hyraxi raider-boys too, slumped against the walls. They have substantial bruising all over their chests, like they both took sledgehammer hits to the ribs.

		The door to the north is labeled “ENGINE. AUTHORIZED EMPLOYEES ONLY.” The very front of the huge, armored train - and it looks like somebody got there ahead of you."])))
(0,it.zI)(Je||(Je=(0,et.Z)(["There’s a handful of terrified-looking divrani huddled up between the seats, but no hyraxi raiders to be seen... on this car."])))

"+(0,it.zI)(Qe||(Qe=(0,et.Z)(["You can hear muffled gunshots and crashing metal in the direction of the train’s rear, back the way [rival.name] and [rival.hisHer] little party are... but no commotion ahead. Yet."])))

(0,it.zI)(Xe||(Xe=(0,et.Z)(["The galley car contains a fully-stocked kitchen, an automated chef, and several cloth-covered tables for dining. It was probably nice, before the whole gunfight and melee - now the place is pock-marked with bullet holes, dented from Johana’s concussion pistol, and broken wine bottles are scattered everywhere. The Hyraxi Alpha is bent over the bar where you left him, and his underlings have either scattered or are cowering."])))
(This is as far as the main story quest content goes for now. More coming soon (tm))

(0,it.zI)($e||($e=(0,et.Z)(["You are standing in a tall, sunlit room, meeting hall-sized in dimension. The light cascades down from rents in the convex, gun-metal ceiling, the result of natural wear and tear on the ruined complex. Where the beams fall, metal girders have been built, and they are festooned in greenery - crawling vines and bright, orchid-like flowers. The flora is energetic and quite wild, its tendrils reaching upward and outward to colonize some of the roof and floor space as well. The walls are draped with woven tapestries of the Kihan faith, displaying its cog symbol, its tenets, its saints and the beatific face of its god in a rainbow of colors.\n\t\t\t\n\t\t\tLittering this miniature paradise are divrani, as many as 30 of them. The females are dressed in crimson headscarves opened at the back to allow their plant-braids out, the males in red, felt collars; as is common with the stumpy green reptile folk, their everything else is allowed to loll freely all the way out there."])))
